role: A React overlay surface that renders the live Company noticeboard as grouped sections by delegating layout and list rendering to a runtime DNA archetype system.
capabilities: Listen for `board:open` window event to open
Close via Escape key, scrim click, or close button
Fetch live board rows through boardStore
Render grouped board sections through DNA `faceRecord.boardRecord`, `org.boardGroups`, and `renderArchetype`
Gracefully degrade when DNA runtime, archetype, or adapter is unavailable
Display loading, error-with-retry, and empty states
substance_why: It wires real open/close interactions, loads live data, delegates rendering to DNA with adapter error handling, and provides loading/error/empty UI states.
interactions_present: Close button click closes the overlay
Error state 'Try again' button re-fetches board data
interactions_missing: No item-level row selection or navigation
No search, filter, or grouping controls (groupBy is hardcoded)
No keyboard focus trap for the modal dialog
No retry for DNA render failures beyond a static message
No loading indicator inside the scrollable body while DNA renders rows
performance_risks: Re-runs DNA `boardRecord` and `renderArchetype` on every `rows` change, rebuilding the entire grouped DOM
No virtualization for long scrollable board lists
Fetches `/dna/layouts.json` on each open if the archetype is not already cached
Unconditional `replaceChildren` with DNA-generated HTML may cause layout thrash
reuse_why: Keep the overlay lifecycle, store binding, and state-machine pattern, but replace the DNA archetype rendering contract and CSS class structure with the new design system.
decomposition_hints: Extract a generic overlay shell (scrim, header, close, keyboard handling) from the board-specific body
Extract a `BoardGroups` renderer/adapter that isolates DNA-specific rendering logic
Extract loading, error, and empty states into reusable message components
Move keyboard/focus management into a reusable overlay hook
Separate board data fetching hooks from the view component
